Definition: A user account represents the identity container that allows a person to authenticate and access services, settings, and personal data on a platform.
A piece of digital information such as an article, post, video, or document that can be viewed, searched, or interacted with on a platform.
An item returned by a search query that represents a matching piece of content or resource.
A personal overview page that aggregates recent activity, notifications, and shortcuts to commonly accessed resources.
A feed that displays recent activity across a user’s content, subscriptions, or courses.
A user-created container that stores a group of items for later reference or organization, such as a wishlist or saved items list.
A lightweight positive-preference marker applied to content to indicate interest or save it for quick access.
A published piece of user-generated material such as an article, post, video, or message shared on a platform.
An editable, unpublished version of content that can be modified before publication.
A digital file such as an image, video, or document that can be uploaded, downloaded, or attached to content.
A user-generated written evaluation of a product, service, or item.
A numeric or scalar evaluation used to measure the quality or usefulness of an item.
A user-submitted request for information or help posted to a community for answers.
A response posted to address a question.
A short message attached to a post used for clarification or discussion.
A user-created survey or questionnaire used to gather opinions or feedback from a community.
A container that organizes learning materials, assignments, and communication tools for an educational experience.
An activity or assessment that students must complete as part of a course.
A structured plan of tasks or milestones that guide learners toward completing learning objectives.
A defined learning objective used to measure student progress or mastery.
An opt-in relationship through which posts or updates from a publisher are delivered to subscribers via email.
A message sent to a user to inform them of an event, update, or action related to their account or content.
User-configured settings that determine how and when notifications are delivered for various events or updates.
A persistent query or topic subscription that notifies the user when new matching content appears.
The user-facing representation of an account that includes identity attributes such as name, avatar, avatar frame, and biography.
User-provided details such as email address, phone number, or mailing address used for communication or account recovery.
Settings that determine whether and how a user receives notifications such as invitations, alerts, or updates.
Settings that control the display language or regional locale used in the interface.
A paid subscription or access level that grants additional features or content beyond a standard account.
Software that allows customers to select and manage items they intend to purchase before checkout.
A purchase record representing the confirmed transaction after checkout.
A promotional mechanism that reduces the price of items or orders.
A recurring billing arrangement where customers automatically pay for products or services at regular intervals.
An arrangement where customers can exchange an old item for credit toward a new purchase.
A digital code that can be redeemed for goods or services. A e-gift card is purchased and then sent to the recipient email inbox.
A stored payment instrument such as a credit card or digital wallet used to complete purchases.
The process and details related to the shipment of physical goods, including address, instructions, and preferences.
An arrangement where customers collect purchased items from a physical location rather than having them delivered.
An arrangement in which a resource such as a seat, room, or table is held for a user at a specified time.
A scheduled meeting or service time with a specific provider or professional.
A workspace that groups related tasks, artifacts, and discussions for collaborative work.
A generated URL that allows other users to access a resource without direct account permissions.
A storage location where project files and revision history are maintained.
A parallel version of a repository used to develop changes independently before merging.
A tracked item used to report bugs or discuss improvements in a repository.
A proposal to merge changes from one branch or fork into another branch.
A collaborative documentation page associated with a repository.
A conversation space used for community questions and discussions related to a project.
A simple way to share code snippets using a Git repository hosted by GitHub.
A digital artifact stored in a repository that can be created or modified.
A subscription relationship where a user chooses to receive updates from another user, channel, or topic.
A direct communication sent from one user to another or a group, such as a private message or direct message.
